description_long: This object is a set of three vertically oriented traffic signal housings, each mounted on a rectangular backplate. The front face displays three columns of circular signal lights, with the left and center columns showing illuminated red left-turn arrows, while the right column is unlit. The housing appears to be made of a dark, matte-finished metal or plastic, with each signal light encased in a cylindrical visor to shield from sunlight. The back and sides are solid and featureless, suggesting a robust, weather-resistant construction. The overall design is typical for urban intersections, providing clear directional signals for vehicular traffic.
description: A triple-column traffic light with vertical stacks of circular signal lights, two of which display red left-turn arrows. The housing is dark and robust, with cylindrical visors over each light.
description_view_0: Front view showing three columns of signal lights, with the left and center columns displaying illuminated red left-turn arrows.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the housing and the protruding cylindrical visors of the signal lights.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the solid, featureless backplates of the three traffic light housings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the depth and cylindrical visors from the other side.
